-module(solution).
-export([solve/2]).

%% Helper function to generate all subsets of a list, returning a list of {XOR_sum, Size} pairs.
%% This runs in O(2^N) time relative to the input list length N.
generate_list(List) ->
    generate_list_rec(List, 0, 0, []).

generate_list_rec([], X, S, Acc) ->
    [{X, S} | Acc];
generate_list_rec([H|T], X, S, Acc) ->
    % Exclude H
    Exclude = generate_list_rec(T, X, S, Acc),
    % Include H
    Include = generate_list_rec(T, X `xor` H, S + 1, []).
    Exclude ++ Include.

%% Helper function to generate a map where keys are XOR sums and values are the maximum size 
%% of a subset achieving that XOR sum. Uses maps: module for efficient updates.
generate_subsets_map(List) ->
    % Initial map: XOR sum 0 achieved with size 0
    InitialMap = maps:singleton(0, 0),
    
    % Fold over the list, updating the map at each step
    maps:foldl(fun(H, M) -> 
        update_map(H, M)
    end, InitialMap, List).

update_map(H, M) ->
    % Generate new entries: for every {X, S} in M, create {X^H, S+1}
    NewEntries = maps:fold(fun(X, S) -> {X `xor` H, S + 1} end, M, []),
    
    % Merge M and NewEntries, ensuring we keep the maximum size for any given XOR sum.
    maps:fold(fun({X_new, S_new}) -> 
        case maps:lookup(X_new, M) of
            {ok, S_old} -> 
                MaxS = max(S_new, S_old),
                maps:insert(X_new, MaxS, M)
            error -> 
                maps:insert(X_new, S_new, M)
        end
    end, M, NewEntries).

%% Main solver function
solve(Nums, Target) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    N_A = N div 2,
    
    % Split array
    {A, B} = lists:split(Nums, N_A),
    
    % Step 1: Generate MapA (XOR_sum -> Max_size for subset of A)
    MapA = generate_subsets_map(A),
    
    % Step 2: Generate all subsets of B
    SubsetsB = generate_list(B),
    
    % Step 3: Find K_max (Maximum total size)
    K_max = find_k_max(SubsetsB, MapA, Target, 0),
    
    % Step 4: Determine minimum removals
    case K_max of
        -1 -> 
            -1;
        K_found -> 
            N - K_found
    end.

%% Finds the maximum total size K_max by pairing subsets of B with suitable subsets of A.
find_k_max([], _MapA, _Target, Max) -> Max;
find_k_max([{XB, SB} | RestB], MapA, Target, Max) ->
    % Required XOR sum from A
    XA_req = Target `xor` XB,
    
    case maps:lookup(XA_req, MapA) of
        {ok, SA} ->
            TotalSize = SA + SB,
            NewMax = max(Max, TotalSize),
            find_k_max(RestB, MapA, Target, NewMax);
        error ->
            % No matching subset in A
            find_k_max(RestB, MapA, Target, Max)
    end.

-module(solution).
-export([solve/2]).

%% Helper function to generate all subsets of a list, returning a list of {XOR_sum, Size} pairs.
%% This runs in O(2^N) time relative to the input list length N.
generate_list(List) ->
    generate_list_rec(List, 0, 0, []).

generate_list_rec([], X, S, Acc) ->
    [{X, S} | Acc];
generate_list_rec([H|T], X, S, Acc) ->
    % Exclude H
    Exclude = generate_list_rec(T, X, S, Acc),
    % Include H
    Include = generate_list_rec(T, X `xor` H, S + 1, []).
    Exclude ++ Include.

%% Helper function to generate a map where keys are XOR sums and values are the maximum size 
%% of a subset achieving that XOR sum. Uses maps: module for efficient updates.
generate_subsets_map(List) ->
    % Initial map: XOR sum 0 achieved with size 0
    InitialMap = maps:singleton(0, 0),
    
    % Fold over the list, updating the map at each step
    maps:foldl(fun(H, M) -> 
        update_map(H, M)
    end, InitialMap, List).

update_map(H, M) ->
    % Generate new entries: for every {X, S} in M, create {X^H, S+1}
    NewEntries = maps:fold(fun(X, S) -> {X `xor` H, S + 1} end, M, []),
    
    % Merge M and NewEntries, ensuring we keep the maximum size for any given XOR sum.
    maps:fold(fun({X_new, S_new}) -> 
        case maps:lookup(X_new, M) of
            {ok, S_old} -> 
                MaxS = max(S_new, S_old),
                maps:insert(X_new, MaxS, M)
            error -> 
                maps:insert(X_new, S_new, M)
        end
    end, M, NewEntries).

%% Main solver function
solve(Nums, Target) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    N_A = N div 2,
    
    % Split array
    {A, B} = lists:split(Nums, N_A),
    
    % Step 1: Generate MapA (XOR_sum -> Max_size for subset of A)
    MapA = generate_subsets_map(A),
    
    % Step 2: Generate all subsets of B
    SubsetsB = generate_list(B),
    
    % Step 3: Find K_max (Maximum total size)
    K_max = find_k_max(SubsetsB, MapA, Target, 0),
    
    % Step 4: Determine minimum removals
    case K_max of
        -1 -> 
            -1;
        K_found -> 
            N - K_found
    end.

%% Finds the maximum total size K_max by pairing subsets of B with suitable subsets of A.
find_k_max([], _MapA, _Target, Max) -> Max;
find_k_max([{XB, SB} | RestB], MapA, Target, Max) ->
    % Required XOR sum from A
    XA_req = Target `xor` XB,
    
    case maps:lookup(XA_req, MapA) of
        {ok, SA} ->
            TotalSize = SA + SB,
            NewMax = max(Max, TotalSize),
            find_k_max(RestB, MapA, Target, NewMax);
        error ->
            % No matching subset in A
            find_k_max(RestB, MapA, Target, Max)
    end.
